reasoning:
The Portland Trail Blazers entered the 2025 season as a rebuilding team with an over/under win total of 22.5, and extremely long odds to win the NBA Cup (+27000 or ~0.4% implied probability). The Polymarket price on Oct 30, 2025, right before the NBA Cup started, was 1%. 

On October 31, 2025, they pulled off an upset, defeating the group-favorite Denver Nuggets 109-107 in their first NBA Cup game. This makes them 1-0 in group play and significantly improves their chances of potentially winning their group. However, their subsequent Cup games are against strong opponents like the Houston Rockets and Golden State Warriors. Even if they improbably advance past the group stage, they remain a weak team and would have to win three consecutive single-elimination games against the best teams in the NBA in the knockout rounds. 

Because of their fundamental weakness as a team, winning one group stage game does not drastically alter their chances to win the entire tournament, moving their objective true odds perhaps from 0.5% to 1.5%. A 2% estimate appropriately reflects their status as heavy underdogs while accounting for their opening upset.
